seoMetaTitle: Power Integrations Technical Support | Design Guides & FAQs | LiTong
seoMetaDescription: Power Integrations technical support resources including selection guides, application notes, troubleshooting guides, and product reviews. Expert FAE assistance available.

faqs.question: What technical support resources are available for Power Integrations products?
faqs.answer: LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive technical support for Power Integrations products: 1) Selection Guides - Detailed guides for choosing the right product for your application, 2) Application Notes - In-depth technical documentation covering design considerations, 3) Troubleshooting Guides - Solutions for common issues and debugging tips, 4) Product Reviews - Detailed technical analysis and performance characterization, 5) PI Expert Software - Automated design tool for power supply optimization, 6) FAE Support - Direct access to our experienced Field Application Engineers for personalized assistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our support articles or contact our FAE team for personalized technical assistance.

faqs.question: How do I get help with Power Integrations product design?
faqs.answer: Getting design help for Power Integrations products is easy: 1) Start with PI Expert software for automated design and optimization, 2) Review our application notes and reference designs for similar applications, 3) Read our troubleshooting guides for common issues, 4) Contact our FAE team via email or phone for personalized support, 5) Request design review services for critical applications. Our FAE team has extensive experience with Power Integrations products and can help with schematic review, layout optimization, and debugging.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our self-service resources first, then contact FAE for personalized design assistance.

articles.title: How to Select Power Integrations AC-DC Converters

author.name: Michael Chen
author.title: Senior FAE - Power Systems
author.bio: Michael Chen is a Senior Field Application Engineer with 10 years of experience in power electronics and AC-DC converter design. He specializes in high-efficiency power supplies and has supported hundreds of customer designs using Power Integrations products.

articles.summary: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right Power Integrations AC-DC converter for your application. Covers product families, selection criteria, and design considerations.
articles.content: Selecting the right AC-DC converter is critical for power supply performance and cost. Power Integrations offers several product families optimized for different applications. This guide will help you navigate the selection process and choose the optimal solution for your needs.

contentSections.heading: Understanding Power Integrations Product Families
content: Power Integrations offers four main AC-DC converter families:
1. InnoSwitch Family - Highest efficiency with integrated synchronous rectification and FluxLink feedback. Ideal for designs requiring >90% efficiency and compact size.
2. LinkSwitch Family - Cost-effective solutions for low-power applications. Simple design with minimal external components.
3. TOPSwitch Family - Higher power applications up to 250W with excellent standby performance.
4. Hiper Family - High-power LLC resonant converters for applications requiring highest efficiency.

contentSections.heading: Key Selection Criteria
content: When selecting an AC-DC converter, consider these factors:
1. Output Power - Determine your maximum power requirement and select a device with adequate margin.
2. Efficiency Requirements - Higher efficiency reduces thermal management needs and operating costs.
3. Size Constraints - Integration level affects overall solution size.
4. Cost Targets - Balance performance requirements with cost constraints.
5. Standby Power - Important for always-connected applications.

contentSections.heading: Application-Specific Recommendations
content: Different applications have unique requirements:
- Mobile Chargers: LinkSwitch-TN for cost, InnoSwitch for high efficiency
- Appliance Power: InnoSwitch for reliability and efficiency
- Industrial Power: TOPSwitch or Hiper for higher power levels
- LED Drivers: LYTSwitch or LinkSwitch-PH for specific lighting requirements

faeInsights.title: FAE Tips for AC-DC Converter Selection
faeInsights.content: My top recommendation is to start with PI Expert software - it will guide you to the right product family based on your specifications. For high-volume products, always consider the total cost of ownership including efficiency and reliability, not just the IC cost. InnoSwitch pays for itself quickly in energy savings.
faeInsights.insight: My top recommendation is to start with PI Expert software - it will guide you to the right product f...
faeInsights.logic: This approach was selected based on extensive field experience and customer feedback.
keyTakeaways: Follow design guidelines for best results
bestPractices: Use PI Expert for initial design

articles.title: How to Select Power Integrations LED Drivers

author.name: Sarah Liu
author.title: FAE - Lighting Applications
author.bio: Sarah Liu is a Field Application Engineer specializing in LED lighting applications with 8 years of experience. She has extensive knowledge of LED driver design and dimming technologies.

articles.summary: Complete guide to selecting Power Integrations LED drivers. Covers dimming options, power factor requirements, and application-specific recommendations.
articles.content: LED driver selection involves balancing multiple requirements including efficiency, dimming performance, power factor, and cost. This guide helps you navigate the selection process for optimal lighting solutions.

contentSections.heading: LED Driver Product Families
content: Power Integrations offers two main LED driver families:
1. LYTSwitch Family - Optimized for phase-cut dimming with standard wall dimmers. Excellent dimming performance down to 1% brightness.
2. LinkSwitch-PH Family - Single-stage high-PF design with 0-10V and PWM dimming for commercial applications.
Choose LYTSwitch for residential retrofit lamps and LinkSwitch-PH for commercial lighting with control systems.

contentSections.heading: Dimming Considerations
content: Dimming requirements drive product selection:
- Phase-cut dimming: Use LYTSwitch for compatibility with existing wall dimmers
- 0-10V dimming: Use LinkSwitch-PH for commercial control systems
- PWM dimming: LinkSwitch-PH supports PWM for digital control
- No dimming: Either family works, select based on other requirements

contentSections.heading: Power Factor Requirements
content: Power factor is critical for commercial lighting:
- Residential: PF > 0.7 typically acceptable
- Commercial: PF > 0.9 often required, use LinkSwitch-PH
- IEC 61000-3-2: LinkSwitch-PH meets Class C requirements
- Energy codes: Many require high PF for commercial installations

faeInsights.title: FAE Tips for LED Driver Selection
faeInsights.content: Always verify dimming compatibility with your specific dimmer switches during prototyping. Dimming performance varies significantly between dimmer brands. For commercial projects, confirm power factor requirements early - they often dictate product family selection.
faeInsights.insight: Always verify dimming compatibility with your specific dimmer switches during prototyping. Dimming p...

keyTakeaways: Follow design guidelines for best results

articles.title: PI Expert Design Software Guide

author.name: David Wang
author.title: FAE - Design Automation
author.bio: David Wang is a Field Application Engineer specializing in design automation and software tools with 6 years of experience. He is an expert in PI Expert software and power supply optimization.

articles.summary: Complete guide to using PI Expert design software for automated power supply design with Power Integrations products.
articles.content: PI Expert is Power Integrations' automated design software that simplifies power supply design. This guide covers software features, design workflow, and optimization techniques.

contentSections.heading: Getting Started with PI Expert
content: PI Expert Online is a web-based design tool accessible at power.com:
1. Create free account on Power Integrations website
2. Enter your design specifications - input voltage, output voltage/current
3. Select product family or let software recommend
4. Review optimized design with transformer specs and BOM
5. Download design report with schematics and layout guidelines

contentSections.heading: Design Optimization Features
content: PI Expert provides comprehensive optimization:
- Transformer design with core selection and winding specs
- Component selection optimized for cost and performance
- Efficiency estimation across load range
- Thermal analysis with temperature predictions
- EMI filter design recommendations
- Design iteration for multiple optimization targets

contentSections.heading: Advanced Features
content: Advanced capabilities for experienced designers:
- Custom transformer specification
- Multi-output design support
- Sensitivity analysis for component variations
- Design comparison between product families
- Export to SPICE for simulation
- Batch design for parametric analysis

faeInsights.title: FAE Tips for PI Expert
faeInsights.content: Always use PI Expert as your starting point - it will save hours of manual calculations. The transformer specifications are particularly valuable and accurate. For critical designs, run sensitivity analysis to understand component tolerances.
faeInsights.insight: Always use PI Expert as your starting point - it will save hours of manual calculations. The transfo...

keyTakeaways: Follow design guidelines for best results
bestPractices: Use PI Expert for initial design

articles.title: Troubleshooting Power Supply Issues

author.name: Robert Chen
author.title: Senior FAE - Applications
author.bio: Robert Chen is a Senior Field Application Engineer with 12 years of experience in power supply design and troubleshooting. He has debugged thousands of designs across various applications.

articles.summary: Practical troubleshooting guide for common power supply issues including startup problems, instability, thermal issues, and EMI failures.
articles.content: Power supply troubleshooting requires systematic approach and understanding of common failure modes. This guide provides practical solutions for the most frequently encountered issues.

contentSections.heading: No Output or Startup Issues
content: Common causes and solutions for no output:
1. Check input voltage - verify AC input and rectified DC
2. Verify IC supply - measure VCC voltage at IC pin
3. Check for shorts - output diode, capacitor, transformer
4. Review transformer polarity - incorrect phasing prevents startup
5. Inspect for solder bridges - especially on small packages
6. Verify feedback circuit - open feedback can prevent startup

contentSections.heading: Output Instability and Ripple
content: Solutions for unstable output or excessive ripple:
- Insufficient output capacitance - add capacitance or use lower ESR caps
- Feedback loop instability - check compensation components
- Transformer saturation - verify turns ratio and core gap
- Layout issues - minimize high-current loop inductance
- Input filter resonance - adjust input capacitor values

contentSections.heading: Thermal and Efficiency Issues
content: Addressing overheating and poor efficiency:
- Check switching frequency - higher frequency increases losses
- Verify snubber design - excessive dissipation indicates issues
- Review rectifier selection - fast recovery reduces losses
- Inspect transformer - core losses and copper losses
- Measure actual efficiency - compare to datasheet values

faeInsights.title: FAE Troubleshooting Tips
faeInsights.content: Always start with the basics - input voltage, output shorts, and IC supply. 80% of issues are simple mistakes. Use a systematic approach and document measurements. Thermal camera is invaluable for finding hot spots.
faeInsights.insight: Always start with the basics - input voltage, output shorts, and IC supply. 80% of issues are simple...
faeInsights.logic: This approach was selected based on extensive field experience and customer feedback.
keyTakeaways: Follow design guidelines for best results
bestPractices: Use PI Expert for initial design

articles.title: InnoSwitch3-CP INN3675C Technical Review

author.name: Dr. Alex Wang
author.title: Principal FAE - Power Electronics
author.bio: Dr. Alex Wang is a Principal Field Application Engineer with 15 years of experience in power electronics. He holds a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering and specializes in high-frequency power conversion.

articles.summary: In-depth technical review of InnoSwitch3-CP INN3675C including electrical characterization, thermal performance, and reliability testing.
articles.content: The INN3675C represents the state-of-the-art in integrated flyback converters. This technical review presents comprehensive test data and analysis of its performance characteristics.

contentSections.heading: Electrical Characterization
content: Comprehensive electrical testing results:
- Efficiency: 93.2% at 230V AC, 35W output (12V/2.9A)
- Standby power: 18mW at 230V AC, no load
- Load regulation: ±0.5% from 10% to 100% load
- Line regulation: ±0.3% across 90-264V AC input
- Switching frequency: 66kHz at full load
- EMI: Meets EN55022 Class B with 6dB margin

contentSections.heading: Thermal Performance
content: Thermal testing under various conditions:
- Ambient 25°C: IC temperature 78°C at full load
- Ambient 50°C: IC temperature 105°C at full load
- Thermal resistance: 45°C/W with recommended copper area
- Thermal shutdown: Activates at 142°C, hysteresis 20°C
- Continuous operation: Verified 1000 hours at 50°C ambient

contentSections.heading: Reliability and Protection
content: Protection feature testing:
- OVP: Triggers at 125% nominal voltage, response <100μs
- OCP: Cycle-by-cycle limiting at 120% rated current
- OTP: Accurate ±5°C threshold, reliable operation
- Short circuit: Hiccup mode with auto-recovery
- Surge: Withstands 2kV differential, 4kV common-mode

faeInsights.title: Technical Assessment
faeInsights.content: The INN3675C delivers on all performance claims. Efficiency is exceptional, thermal performance is excellent with proper layout, and protection features work reliably. The FluxLink feedback provides better transient response than optocoupler designs. Highly recommended for high-performance applications.
